calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jill Ernst, via Zoom, for Regina Bersaglieri, Personal Representative.

Counsel reports that all taxes have been filed and paid, except for the decedent's joint tax returns which are pending. Counsel advises that a $100k reserve may be too much and asks that the court consider lowering the amount of the reserve to $50k. The court advises that it will consider her request.

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Continued To Apr-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thea Eliot appeared by zoom for and with Kathryn Bersaglieri by zoom.
Gina Bersaglieri appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Ms. Eliot stated that they had discussion with Judge Wiss as to options to move this process forward, that Gina is the sole Beneficiary and if anyone has an interest in moving this forward it would be Gina, and that Gina and Kathryn have spoken, and they would like to substitute Gina in as the Administrator. Gina stated that she is happy to jump in and help, and that she is motivated to get this done. Ms. Eliot stated that she will represent Gina if it is acceptable to all parties. Kathryn and Gina indicated that it is acceptable to them. Discussion was held regarding Kathryn resigning and Gina petitioning to be appointed. Kathryn stated that she met with the Appraiser about two weeks ago at the location where 10 cars are located, that the Appraiser has started the process, that they have arranged for the Appraiser to take a look at the other vehicles and that there are twenty cars all together at various garages throughout the City. Ms. Eliot stated that Amy Harrington indicated that because the cars are considered classics, she wanted a separate Appraiser. Status on suspension of the Administrator and the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration are continued to April 13, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar. The Court on its own motion advances the hearing date on the Motion to Withdraw as Counsel from April 14, 2021 to April 13, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Eliot is to provide courtesy copies of her filings by April 6, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Continued To Mar-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thea Eliot appeared by zoom for Kathryn Bersaglieri.

The Court asked Ms. Eliot if there has been any cooperation from the Administrator. Ms. Eliot informed the Court that she filed a motion to withdraw as counsel, and stated that she has left text messages and sent two letters via fed ex with forms, but there has been no response what-so-ever. The Court asked if the Administrator's Daughter could assist with getting the Administrator to cooperate. Ms. Eliot stated that she has kind of worked behind the scenes with the Daughter, but the Daughter expressed that it is affecting the relationship with her Mother. The Court stated that it is worried about the estate. Ms. Eliot pointed that a spousal property order had been issued some time ago and that the sole Beneficiary of estate is the Daughter. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Kathryn Bersaglieri to appear on March 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why she is not cooperating with her Attorney to close out this estate. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration and status on suspension of the Administrator are also continued to that date. A copy of the order to appear will also be sent to the Daughter (Regina Bersaglieri).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Continued To Oct-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thea Eliot appeared by zoom for Kathryn Bersaglieri (Administrator).

The Court stated that is should have had a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration with a request for additional time to administer the estate, that it does not have either one, and there is no inventory and appraisal. Ms. Eliot stated that she did not receive notice of this hearing until late Friday afternoon. The Court reminded Ms. Eliot that hearing dates on status on close of estates are set at the time when the personal representatives are appointed and stated that she should have been aware of it. Ms. Eliot apologized to the Court stating that it fell through the cracks, that the surviving spouse has had pretty significant grief and that she had trouble connecting with her. Ms. Eliot also stated that the Administrator had not been responsive to e-mails or phone calls, that she sent the inventory and appraisal to the Administrator in May of 2019, that she made multiple follow ups, but the Administrator did not respond, that she was able to reach the Administrator yesterday, that her Client is willing to move this forward, and that she will follow up and call her Client tomorrow. The Court stated that this estate has been open for three years and that is tempted to suspend the Administrator. The Court also informed Ms. Eliot that within three weeks, she must have either one of the above-mentioned petitions on file, and that the inventory and appraisal must also be filed. Status on close of estate is continued to October 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on suspension of the Administrator is also set for that same date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
